description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a wooden canopy supported by two sturdy posts. The canopy is topped with a blue-tiled, gabled roof, providing shelter for the well opening below. A wooden crank mechanism is attached to one side, likely used for drawing water, and a wooden bucket sits nearby on the ground. The stonework appears weathered and robust, suggesting durability and age, while the wooden elements show a rustic, hand-crafted quality. The well is set on a patch of earth with some grass, indicating an outdoor placement. The overall design is functional, with minimal ornamentation, focusing on utility and protection from the elements.
description: A circular stone water well with a blue-tiled wooden canopy, crank mechanism, and a wooden bucket, set on earthy ground. The design is rustic and utilitarian.
description_view_0: Front view showing the well's stone base, wooden posts, blue-tiled roof, and a wooden bucket placed to the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the open well mouth, crank mechanism, and the thickness of the stone wall.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the canopy and the stone base, with the bucket less visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a clear look at the well opening, crank, and the bucket now on the right.
attribute_well_cover_design: Gabled wooden canopy with blue ceramic tiles, open at the bottom, no lid or slab covering the well mouth.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Visible wooden crank and rope mechanism with a wooden bucket placed beside the well.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately 0.8 meters high, supporting a wooden canopy.
